title: Generated Project Contract
objective: Make the generated project include the legal and workflow assets promised by the onboarding docs, without overwriting project-owned files.
description: The current quick start promises a portable MIT license and an OSS-ready baseline, but `init-docs.sh` only generates docs, specs, scripts, and `package.json`. This slice defines and implements the default generated-file contract so a new adopter gets a coherent starting point instead of a partial scaffold.
must: Teach `init-docs.sh` to copy the default legal and OSS workflow assets when they are missing in the target repo
Keep the copy behavior non-destructive: existing user-owned files are preserved and reported as skipped
Define exactly which files are generated by default versus which ones remain optional in the onboarding docs
Ensure the generated project receives a dedicated Quiver PR template and docs workflow contract where applicable
not_included: Publishing Quiver as an npm package
Automatic migration of user-owned CI workflows
acceptance: A brand-new target repo initialized with Quiver receives the legal and OSS baseline assets documented by the quick start
If the target repo already has files such as `LICENSE` or `.github/pull_request_template.md`, `init-docs.sh` preserves them and reports the skip clearly
The onboarding docs no longer promise files that the generated project does not actually receive
tests: Fresh repo init smoke test for generated assets
assumptions: Generated legal and community-health files should be defaults, not mandatory overwrites
A dedicated Quiver docs workflow can coexist with project-specific CI
